faq.q1: What is a smart contract transaction parser?
faq.a1: A smart contract transaction parser is a tool that decodes the raw calldata of EVM blockchain transactions, revealing the function name, input parameters (with names, types, and values), gas consumption, and execution status. It helps developers and analysts understand what a transaction actually did on-chain.
faq.a2: We support 7 major EVM-compatible chains: Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain (BSC), Polygon, Arbitrum One, Optimism, Base, and Avalanche C-Chain. Each chain has automatic ABI resolution via block explorer APIs, Sourcify, and the 4bytes signature database.
faq.q3: How does automatic ABI resolution work?
faq.a3: The tool uses a waterfall pipeline to resolve contract ABIs: first it checks if the contract is a proxy and resolves the implementation, then tries the chain's block explorer API (Etherscan, etc.), followed by Sourcify's verified source repository, and finally the 4bytes function signature database. If all fail, you can manually paste the ABI.
